_note: Weekday (vara) mantra pages. Weekday→planet is [confirmed classical] (Surya Siddhanta vara order). Weekday→deity is [variable by tradition]. Each day's primary mantra references the ruling planet's name mantra (see planets.json / _canonical_bija_vedic.json) plus the presiding deity's salutation. Devanagari is homoglyph-gated by validate_mantra.mjs.

days.whatIs: Sunday (Ravivar) is the weekday ruled by Surya, the Sun, in the classical vara order of the Surya Siddhanta. It is traditionally dedicated to Sun worship and, by widespread regional custom, to forms of Vishnu and Surya-Narayana. Devotees recite the Surya mantra "Om Suryaya Namah" at sunrise facing east, often observing a Ravivar vrat with a single meal and the avoidance of salt or oil. The day is held auspicious for matters of health, vitality, authority, and self-confidence — the significations of the Sun.

mantras.source: Vedic planetary mantra tradition; vara = Surya Siddhanta
mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of the Sun, the ruler of Sunday; recited at sunrise facing east.

benefits: Traditionally chanted for vitality, health, and confidence
Favoured for matters of authority and self-discipline
vidhi.impact: Sunday practice aligns the week's start with solar significations — vigour and clarity of purpose.

pujaModes.minimal: 108 japa of Om Suryaya Namah at sunrise with water Arghya
pujaModes.full: Ravivar vrat with Surya puja and Aditya Hridayam

guruDiksha.note: The name mantra is open to all.

faqs.question: Which mantra is chanted on Sunday?
faqs.answer: The Surya (Sun) mantra "Om Suryaya Namah", since Sunday (Ravivar) is ruled by the Sun. It is chanted at sunrise facing east.

faqs.question: Which god is worshipped on Sunday?
faqs.answer: Surya the Sun-god is the classical ruler of Sunday; by regional tradition, forms of Vishnu and Surya-Narayana are also worshipped.

days.whatIs: Monday (Somvar) is the weekday ruled by Chandra, the Moon, in the classical vara order. It is most widely dedicated to Lord Shiva, whose worship on Mondays — and especially during the Shravan month and on Solah Somvar vrats — is among the most popular Hindu observances. Devotees may chant the Moon mantra "Om Chandraya Namah" for emotional calm, or the Shiva mantra "Om Namah Shivaya" for the presiding deity. The day favours matters of the mind, peace, motherhood, and devotion.

mantras.source: Vedic planetary mantra tradition; vara = Surya Siddhanta
mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of the Moon, the ruler of Monday; chanted for calm of mind.
The five-syllable mantra of Shiva, the presiding deity of Monday worship.

benefits: Traditionally chanted for peace of mind and emotional balance
Favoured for Shiva devotion, marriage, and well-being
vidhi.impact: Monday practice steadies the mind (Moon) and invokes Shiva's grace.

pujaModes.medium: Japa + jalabhishek of the Shiva linga
pujaModes.full: Somvar vrat with Rudrabhishek and Shiva Chalisa

guruDiksha.note: Both name mantras are open to all.

faqs.question: Which mantra is chanted on Monday?
faqs.answer: Monday (Somvar) is ruled by the Moon, so "Om Chandraya Namah" is chanted for the planet; most devotees also chant Shiva's "Om Namah Shivaya", as Monday is Shiva's day.

faqs.question: Why is Monday dedicated to Shiva?
faqs.answer: By long tradition Monday is Shiva's day; the Moon (which rules Monday) adorns Shiva's head, linking the planet and the deity. Shravan Mondays and Solah Somvar vrats are especially observed.

days.whatIs: Tuesday (Mangalvar) is the weekday ruled by Mangal, the planet Mars, in the classical vara order. It is most widely dedicated to Hanuman, and in many regions to Devi and Kartikeya. Devotees chant the Mars mantra "Om Bhaumaya Namah" for courage, or the Hanuman mantra and Hanuman Chalisa for the presiding deity. The day favours strength, courage, protection from enemies, and the remediation of Mangal (manglik) dosha.

mantras.source: Vedic planetary mantra tradition; vara = Surya Siddhanta
mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of Mars, the ruler of Tuesday; chanted for courage and energy.
A widely-used Hanuman seed mantra for Tuesday, the day of Hanuman worship.

benefits: Traditionally chanted for courage, strength, and protection
Favoured for Hanuman devotion and Mangal-dosha remedies
vidhi.impact: Tuesday practice channels Mars's energy and invokes Hanuman's protection.

pujaModes.full: Tuesday vrat with Sundarkand and Hanuman puja

guruDiksha.note: Name mantra and Hanuman Chalisa are open to all.

faqs.question: Which mantra is chanted on Tuesday?
faqs.answer: Tuesday (Mangalvar) is ruled by Mars, so "Om Bhaumaya Namah" is chanted; most devotees also recite the Hanuman Chalisa and Hanuman mantra, as Tuesday is Hanuman's day.

faqs.question: Why is Tuesday dedicated to Hanuman?
faqs.answer: Hanuman embodies the courage and strength signified by Mars, the ruler of Tuesday; the day is the principal weekly observance for Hanuman across North India.

days.whatIs: Wednesday (Budhvar) is the weekday ruled by Budha, the planet Mercury, in the classical vara order. It is most widely dedicated to Ganesha, and in the Vaishnava tradition to Vishnu/Krishna. Devotees chant the Mercury mantra "Om Budhaya Namah" for intellect, or the Ganesha mantra "Om Gam Ganapataye Namah" for the presiding deity. The day favours learning, communication, business, and the removal of obstacles.

mantras.source: Vedic planetary mantra tradition; vara = Surya Siddhanta
mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of Mercury, the ruler of Wednesday; chanted for intellect and speech.
The seed mantra of Ganesha, presiding deity of Wednesday, invoked to remove obstacles.

benefits: Traditionally chanted for intellect, learning, and communication
Favoured for Ganesha worship and the removal of obstacles
vidhi.impact: Wednesday practice sharpens Mercury's intellect and invokes Ganesha's grace before new ventures.

pujaModes.minimal: 108 japa of Om Gam Ganapataye Namah

guruDiksha.note: Both mantras are open to all.

faqs.question: Which mantra is chanted on Wednesday?
faqs.answer: Wednesday (Budhvar) is ruled by Mercury, so "Om Budhaya Namah" is chanted; many devotees also chant Ganesha's "Om Gam Ganapataye Namah", as Wednesday is Ganesha's day.

faqs.question: Which god is worshipped on Wednesday?
faqs.answer: Ganesha is the most widely worshipped deity on Wednesday; the Vaishnava tradition also dedicates the day to Vishnu and Krishna.

days.whatIs: Thursday (Guruvar or Brihaspativar) is the weekday ruled by Guru, the planet Jupiter, in the classical vara order. It is most widely dedicated to Vishnu and to Dattatreya and Sai Baba in various traditions. Devotees chant the Jupiter mantra "Om Gurave Namah" for wisdom, or the Vishnu mantra "Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya" for the presiding deity. The day favours wisdom, education, progeny, marriage, and prosperity.

mantras.source: Vedic planetary mantra tradition; vara = Surya Siddhanta
mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of Jupiter, the ruler of Thursday; chanted for wisdom and fortune.
The twelve-syllable mantra of Vishnu, presiding deity of Thursday.

benefits: Traditionally chanted for wisdom, education, and prosperity
Favoured for Vishnu devotion, marriage, and progeny
vidhi.impact: Thursday practice draws Jupiter's wisdom and Vishnu's grace for growth and good fortune.

pujaModes.minimal: 108 japa of Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya
pujaModes.full: Guruvar vrat with Vishnu/Brihaspati puja and yellow offerings

guruDiksha.note: Both mantras are open to all.

faqs.question: Which mantra is chanted on Thursday?
faqs.answer: Thursday (Guruvar) is ruled by Jupiter, so "Om Gurave Namah" is chanted; most devotees also chant Vishnu's "Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya", as Thursday is Vishnu's day.

faqs.question: Which god is worshipped on Thursday?
faqs.answer: Vishnu is the most widely worshipped deity on Thursday; Dattatreya and Sai Baba are also venerated in their respective traditions.

days.whatIs: Friday (Shukravar) is the weekday ruled by Shukra, the planet Venus, in the classical vara order. It is most widely dedicated to the Goddess — Lakshmi for wealth and Durga/Santoshi Maa in their vrat traditions. Devotees chant the Venus mantra "Om Shukraya Namah" for harmony, or the Lakshmi mantra "Om Shreem Mahalakshmyai Namah" for the presiding goddess. The day favours wealth, love, beauty, marital harmony, and comfort.

mantras.source: Vedic planetary mantra tradition; vara = Surya Siddhanta
mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of Venus, the ruler of Friday; chanted for harmony and comfort.
The seed mantra of Mahalakshmi, presiding goddess of Friday, invoked for wealth and abundance.

benefits: Traditionally chanted for wealth, harmony, and marital happiness
Favoured for Lakshmi and Durga worship
vidhi.impact: Friday practice invokes Venus's grace of harmony and the Goddess's blessings of abundance.

pujaModes.minimal: 108 japa of Om Shreem Mahalakshmyai Namah
pujaModes.full: Friday vrat with Shri Suktam and Lakshmi puja

guruDiksha.note: Both mantras are open to all.

faqs.question: Which mantra is chanted on Friday?
faqs.answer: Friday (Shukravar) is ruled by Venus, so "Om Shukraya Namah" is chanted; most devotees also chant Lakshmi's "Om Shreem Mahalakshmyai Namah", as Friday is the Goddess's day.

faqs.question: Which goddess is worshipped on Friday?
faqs.answer: Lakshmi (for wealth) is the most widely worshipped on Friday; Durga, Santoshi Maa and other Devi forms are also venerated in their vrat traditions.

days.whatIs: Saturday (Shanivar) is the weekday ruled by Shani, the planet Saturn, in the classical vara order. It is most widely dedicated to Shani Dev, and also to Hanuman, who is traditionally held to relieve Saturn's hardships. Devotees chant the Saturn mantra "Om Shanaischaraya Namah" for relief during Sade Sati, often lighting a sesame-oil lamp and serving the needy. The day favours discipline, patience, the easing of obstacles, and karmic remediation.

mantras.source: Vedic planetary mantra tradition; vara = Surya Siddhanta
mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of Saturn, the ruler of Saturday; chanted for patience and relief from hardship.

benefits: Traditionally chanted for relief during Sade Sati and Saturn periods
Favoured for discipline, patience, and Hanuman worship
vidhi.impact: Saturday practice steadies the mind under Saturn's trials and invokes Hanuman's protection.

pujaModes.minimal: 108 japa of Om Shanaischaraya Namah + til-oil lamp
pujaModes.full: Shanivar vrat with Shani puja, til-oil lamp, and anna-daan

guruDiksha.note: The name mantra is open to all.

faqs.question: Which mantra is chanted on Saturday?
faqs.answer: Saturday (Shanivar) is ruled by Saturn, so "Om Shanaischaraya Namah" is chanted; many devotees also recite the Hanuman Chalisa, as Hanuman is held to relieve Saturn's troubles.

faqs.question: Which god is worshipped on Saturday?
faqs.answer: Shani Dev (Saturn) is the principal deity of Saturday; Hanuman is also widely worshipped on this day for protection from Saturn's hardships.
